article_title: Deno 2.8 Ships Six New Subcommands, 3.66x Faster npm Installs, and Chrome DevTools Network Inspection

content_preview.title: Deno 2.8 Ships Six New Subcommands, 3.66x Faster npm Installs, and Chrome DevTools Network Inspection
content_preview.summary: Deno 2.8, released May 22, adds deno audit fix, deno pack, deno ci, deno transpile, deno why, and deno bump-version, while slashing cold npm install times by 3.66x and enabling Chrome DevTools network traffic inspection.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Deno 2.8, released on May 22, 2026, is the runtime's largest minor release to date, adding six new subcommands, cutting cold npm install times from 3,319ms to 906ms, and letting Chrome DevTools inspect Deno's live network traffic, according to the [Deno team's release announcement](https://deno.com/blog/v2.8). The release ships alongside TypeScript 6.0.3 and V8 14.9, and more than doubles the Node.js test suite pass rate compared to Deno 2.7.

## Six New Subcommands

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent technical Briefing covering a comprehensive release. Six subcommands are each clearly described. Performance benchmark numbers are specific and well-presented. Article is 805 words, within Briefing range.
editor_notes.source_verification: Both sources verified from disk snapshots: source-0.html.gz (Deno blog v2.8 — confirms all key specifics: 3,319ms→906ms cold install / 3.66x improvement, 76.4% Node.js test suite pass rate (3,405/4,457), 42% in 2.7, all six subcommands, TypeScript 6.0.3, V8 14.9, 500 commits). source-1.html.gz (GitHub release v2.8.0 — confirms release exists). All benchmarks and feature descriptions confirmed verbatim from official Deno blog.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All benchmark numbers verified: 3,319ms→906ms (3.66x cold install), node:buffer 3.07x, node:http throughput 2.21x, node:crypto scrypt 2.12x, node:http p99 1.75x. Node.js test pass rate 76.4% (3,405/4,457) vs 42% in 2.7 confirmed. All six subcommands confirmed.
editor_notes.originality: No existing Deno 2.8 coverage found. Prior Deno 2.7 article mentioned in body as a proper internal link.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality technical release Briefing with all claims sourced from the official Deno blog. All numeric specifics verified. Approved.
